steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: Look past the prepositional phrase “of each statue's ears” to find the head noun: “the shape” is singular. The comma-enclosed phrase “like the shape of each person's ears” is a parenthetical comparison introduced by “like,” not a coordinating conjunction, so it doesn't add a second subject. The subject is singular.

steps.title: Apply the subject-verb agreement rule
steps.content: A singular subject requires a singular verb. “Is” is the singular form that correctly agrees with “the shape.” “Are” and “have been” are plural; “were” is plural past tense. This matches choice B — B is correct.
